reviews.review_msg: Best Prof in the dept.

She is intelligent and interested in her field of study, but she is extremely monotonous. I could not stay awake because of that. I wish she would be a little more interesting... the course content is so interesting, I think that's how I survived.

One of the best profs I've ever had at SFU! She is passionate about her subject and genuinely interested in what you have to say. She gives clear, well-organized and interesting lectures and reasonable amounts of reading. I'd recommend her any day!

The class was okay and her marking was fair, maybe a bit hard but she's approachable and very willing to help or suggest supplementary materials or films.  There are a lot of little details so make sure you pay attention.
Extremely interesting class, you will take lots and lots of notes.  Less assignments than other upper division history classes and she is a really easy marker.
Her lectures were extremely interesting and the way she structured them made note taking extremely intuitive.  I highly recommend taking a class with her (although be prepared for low midterm marks).

Expect your average history course. The material was fairly easy but the marking was brutal. The marking scheme demanded more out of students than normal, which led to dismal mid-term marks. She wound up scaling up the class, but this is something to keep in mind. When you think you wrote enough, write some more!

The most passionate professor I've ever had! In her lectures, I never stopped writing notes like mad. She even had a way of making otherwise boring material interesting! Any history buff will love her as much as I did.
A great prof. Lectures are sometimes boring but I think it's more because of the content itself rather than the prof. Very approachable and helpful.
She was a great prof although sometimes her voice put me to sleep. I usually stayed awake though, and I learned a lot. Very friendly outside of the lecture hall as well.
She speaks in iambic pentameter. If you don't write notes it puts you to sleep. But if you take notes she's awesome. Her lectures are soooo interesting, especially when she talks about Germany.

I had her 2 years ago and I still think she is amazing.  She inspired me to be a history major.  If you can, take her.  She has the clearest lectures that are so full of information you will be writing like mad!  I can't stress enough how great of a prof she is and I'm lucky to have had her.
Wonderful prof who gets really into what she teaches.  She doesn't go off topic and is very good at what she does.  But her exam was a bit hard

Nadine is so incredibly passionate about history that it comes out in her voice - definitely kept me wide awake in class and writing notes like mad. She answers students' questions clearly and never goes off topic. My only complaint is that we had too much reading and it was sometimes hard to keep up, and my TA was a nightmare. Nadine is beautiful!

Nadine's lectures were awesome! She's very passionate about what she was teaching, even though it's a general "overview" type course.  Even though it was a large lecture she allowed questions and answered them with clear and concise answers. Assignments and tests are fair.

Best prof, very interesting to listen to, even if you do not have a high intrest in her topics, told an interesting story of German history and compelled students to be interested, hard marker but if you work hard she will reward you and she gives very helpful advice on papers. I would take a class with her in a second
Made the class very interesting with very compeling

This prof is the most beautiful historian I have ever met. That alone made the class worth while. But more than that, she made class interesting, and was very clear and concise. If you have an opportunity, definatly take this professor

I rated her clarity as average since this is a seminar class - the students are the one's expected to do the 'teaching', so she is merely a facilitator - though she's a good one. She is helpful, though topics tend to get a bit repetitive - essentially it's three months of talking about the memory of Nazi Germany, nothing else! Above average class.
Great Prof!!
Very engaging speaker, extremely interested in what she is talking about.  Topic super interesting too.  This class is highly recommended
nadine roth is a very competent prof. this course is not challenging, but it is a very good grounding in 20th century european history.
Best looking prof in the department and has the most seductive voice around- that aside, she is clear and fair in her lectures and marking standards.

Nadine is a great prof. Lots of notes, but they end up being very clear. She isn't too tough on her exams and her take-home finals are pretty easy as well. Shes good in tutorials too by actually letting you talk, plus shes nice to look at!

A very nice, approachable woman with a passion for what she is teaching. I definitely loved the class, but I was not happy with all of the guest lecturers that we had to suffer through (they were "auditioning" for a job with SFU) but at least the stuff they covered wasn't on the final!
did all the readings, attended all lectures and still got a B-, she is too hard for an intro course, I am in my fourth year! Don't recommend

Nadine is an inspiring orater.  Her lectures are interesting and she is passioniate when giving them.  Her marking is rather strict for a 1st year course, but Nadine is always open to discuss your grade as long as you have a good argument.  She is beautiful; sit up close if possible.

I cannot say enough about Nadine Roth. She is an amazing professor, the best in the SFU History Dept. She challenges her students, but not to the breaking point, and she is very passionate about her subject matter. Unlike some people below, I find her style (and voice!) incredibly engaging. Very open to questions and diverse opinions. Nadine rocks.
If the subject interests you take this class, although I'd recommend anything with Nadine. Even for you slackers, this should be engaging enough to get into it.
she is a hard and boring prof. I would not recommend people.

Her lectures are hard to follow because her headings are so long, by the time you have them copied down she's said 5 important things you missed. She loves and knows her history, but her voice is just annoying! It's almost a patronizing tone, or one used to sing songs. Nice lady though
Sooooo boring! She made history evil.
No Comments

She comes off like a bit of a hippy at first, but don't be fooled, she is very intelligent and knowledgeable about history. Her lectures were great because she was so passionate. Passionate is really the best word, she speaks with this intensity and interest that really brings the subject to life. I don't think she's really THAT hard.
Smoking hot babe so much so that it is hard to concentrate. Absolute killer on marks. You really, really really  have to work to any better than a C.
Dr. Roth is an amazing teacher! Very passionate abut her subject, a great tutorial leader, and very organized lectures: the whole package. Plus, she is kinda hot in a sexy librarian way.
REALLY HARD.  The format of the class is great and she is totally nice but she is SO HARD.  Hard exams and a hard marker.
Best all round teacher at the school.  Not easy but fair and she's hot.
Awesome Prof... knows her stuff, fair marking... just read sourcebook, you'll be good to go.

Very interesting material. But beware that her testing format makes the material a lot harder than it should be. You have to study all the sourcebook stories which is a chore. Only had 45 mins to complete midterm.

Nadine is WONDERFUL... repeating what has been said: not easiest, but really fair and willing to help and talk to you.  Great prof, knowledgeable, and wonderful lectures prepared weekly.  I'd give anything to take her class again!
Great teacher, very knowledgable about Germany and a sweetheart. Not the easiest teacher but one of the BEST! Take her class if you can, you won't regret it.
Awesome teacher.  Best in the department.  Not the easiest but fair.
Very knowledgeable and interested in the material.  Great powerpoint used in lectures.  I recommend taking a class with her if you can.
awsome teacher.
Nadine is awesome!
Her love of German History is contagious, an excellent instructor, and very easy to talk to.
Loves what she does and it shows
great prof!
Great Teacher
ambivalent.  great readings. poor discussion.
good lecture and good tutorials.  too bad her upper division classes were full
Excellent lecturer... always makes class interesting.
Very good lecturer...
No Comments
